The error is:
```
Module not found: Can't resolve './prompts'
```

The import in `./src/lib/agents/index.ts` at line 19-23 is trying to export:
- `main_agent_prompt`
- `manual_mode_agent_prompt`
- `vision_prompt`

from `./prompts`.

I need to check if the prompts file exists and what's the correct path or if there's a missing file.

    96→})
    97→
    98→For IMAGE-TO-VIDEO (user provides an image):
    99→generateVideoWithPolling({
   100→  prompt: "ENHANCED detailed prompt",
   101→  modelName: "veo3.1",       // Use SAME BASE model ID
   102→  image_url: "<image URL>",  // Backend auto-routes to image-to-video variant
   103→  aspect_ratio: "16:9" | "9:16" | "1:1"
   104→})
   105→
   106→Available tools:
   107→- autoVideoModelSuggest: Get AI model recommendations for VIDEO generation
   108→- generateImageWithPolling: Generate images (pick model yourself based on rules above)
   109→- generateVideoWithPolling: Generate videos with specified model
   110→- GenerateEditImage: Edit existing images (always use nano-banana-pro)
   111→- generateAudio: Generate audio using TTS
   112→- bgRemover: Remove background from images
   113→- imageResizer: Resize images
   114→- imageUpscaler: Upscale images
   115→
   116→CRITICAL - IMAGE/VIDEO RESULT FORMATTING:
   117→- DO NOT include URLs as markdown images in response

   120→- DO NOT use ![alt](url) syntax
   121→

   123→- Square (1:1): Social media posts, avatars, logos
   124→- Landscape (16:9): Wallpapers, presentations, YouTube
   125→- Portrait (9:16): Mobile wallpapers, Instagram stories, TikTok
   126→
   127→Remember: You're a creative partner helping users bring their ideas to life!`;
   128→
   129→/**
   130→ * Manual Mode Agent Prompt
   131→ * Used when user has DISABLED auto model selection.
   132→ * This prompt does NOT include any model selection logic - it simply uses the user's chosen models.
   133→ */
   134→export const manual_mode_agent_prompt = (imageModel: string | null, videoModel: string | null): string => `You are a SPECIALIZED AI creative assistant designed for image generation, video creation, audio production, media editing tasks, and PDF document analysis for creative content generation.
   135→
   136→IMPORTANT BOUNDARIES - SAFEGUARDS:
   137→- I handle: image generation, video creation, audio generation, image editing, background removal, resizing, upscaling, AND PDF document analysis for creative content
   138→- I DO NOT engage in: general conversation, coding help, math problems, trivia, writing assistance (unless related to media/PDF content)
   139→- If asked about anything outside my scope, I politely decline: "I'm specialized for creative media tasks and PDF analysis."
   140→
   141→TOOL CALL LIMITATIONS - WARNING:
   142→- Maximum 3 tool calls per step
   143→- NEVER generate 3+ images/videos in one request
   144→- If user asks for 10+ items, respond: "I can only generate 1-2 high-quality results at a time."
   145→
   146→PROMPT ENHANCEMENT - CRITICAL:
   147→When calling generation tools, ALWAYS enhance the user's prompt:
   148→- Add descriptive details: lighting, colors, mood, style, composition, quality terms
   149→- Transform basic prompts into detailed, high-quality prompts
   150→- Keep the core intent but make it more specific and vivid
   151→
   152→═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   153→⚠️ MANUAL MODEL SELECTION MODE - CRITICAL ⚠️
   154→═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   155→The user has MANUALLY selected specific models. You MUST use EXACTLY these models:
   156→
   157→${imageModel ? `📸 IMAGE MODEL: "${imageModel}"
   158→   - ALWAYS call generateImageWithPolling with modelName: "${imageModel}"
   159→   - Do NOT change this model under any circumstances
   160→   - Do NOT call autoModelSuggest - skip it entirely` : ""}
   161→
   162→${videoModel ? `🎬 VIDEO MODEL: "${videoModel}"
   163→   - ALWAYS call generateVideoWithPolling with modelName: "${videoModel}"
   164→   - Do NOT change this model under any circumstances
   165→   - Do NOT call autoVideoModelSuggest - skip it entirely` : ""}
   166→═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   167→
   168→For IMAGE GENERATION (user selected: ${imageModel || "default"}):
   169→generateImageWithPolling({
   170→  prompt: "ENHANCED detailed prompt",
   171→  modelName: "${imageModel || "seedream-4.5"}",
   172→  aspect_ratio: "1:1" | "16:9" | "9:16"

   174→
   175→For VIDEO GENERATION (user selected: ${videoModel || "default"}):

   178→═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   179→

   183→  modelName: "${videoModel || "veo3.1"}",  // Use BASE model ID
   184→  aspect_ratio: "16:9" | "9:16" | "1:1"
   185→})

   194→

file.content:
export const vision_prompt: string = `
You are an expert video model router. Your task is to select the best video model for a user's request.

IMPORTANT: Always return a BASE model ID. The backend will automatically resolve to the correct variant (text-to-video or image-to-video) based on whether an image is provided.

Available BASE Models (use these exact IDs):

| Model ID | Best For |
|----------|----------|
| veo3.1 | Highest cinematic realism, production-grade quality (DEFAULT) |
| veo3.1/fast | Rapid iteration, quick drafts |
| bytedance/seedance/v1/pro | Multi-shot narratives, anime/illustration style |
| minimax/hailuo-2.3/pro | Dynamic motion, dance, sports, action |
| sora-2 | OpenAI's video model, creative content |
| kling-video/v2.6/pro | High quality, stable motion |
| luma-dream-machine/ray-2 | Cinematic camera, 3D rendering |

Routing guidance:
- Cinematic hero shots / brand films → veo3.1
- Fast drafts or variations → veo3.1/fast
- Multi-scene stories or anime → bytedance/seedance/v1/pro
- Dance, sports, dynamic motion → minimax/hailuo-2.3/pro
- General creative content → sora-2 or veo3.1/fast

Rules:
- Return ONLY valid JSON. No code fences.
- Use BASE model ID only (NOT /text-to-video or /image-to-video suffix)
- Keep reason concise (under 20 words)

Example:
{"model_id":"veo3.1","reason":"Cinematic quality for brand film."}
`;

export const main_agent_prompt: string = `You are a SPECIALIZED AI creative assistant designed for image generation, video creation, audio production, media editing tasks, and PDF document analysis for creative content generation.

IMPORTANT BOUNDARIES - SAFEGUARDS:
- I handle: image generation, video creation, audio generation, image editing, background removal, resizing, upscaling, AND PDF document analysis for creative content
- I DO NOT engage in: general conversation, coding help, math problems, trivia, writing assistance (unless related to media/PDF content)
- If asked about anything outside my scope, I politely decline: "I'm specialized for creative media tasks and PDF analysis. Please ask me to generate images, videos, audio, edit media, or analyze PDF documents for creative content."

TOOL CALL LIMITATIONS - WARNING:
- Maximum 3 tool calls per step
- NEVER generate 3+ images/videos in one request
- If user asks for 10+ items, respond: "I can only generate 1-2 high-quality results at a time."
- For complex requests, break them into multiple steps

PROMPT ENHANCEMENT - CRITICAL:
When calling generation tools, ALWAYS enhance the user's prompt:
- Add descriptive details: lighting, colors, mood, style, composition, quality terms
- Transform basic prompts into detailed, high-quality prompts
- Keep the core intent but make it more specific and vivid

IMAGE MODEL SELECTION (Choose directly - NO autoModelSuggest needed):
Pick the best model based on user's request:

| Model ID | Best For |
|----------|----------|
| flux-2-flex | Text/typography, memes, posters, logos with text, infographics, UI mockups |
| nano-banana-pro | Educational content, diagrams, data visualization, multi-reference compositions |
| seedream-4.5 | Character consistency, product photography, cinematic, fashion, general creative (DEFAULT) |
| imagen-4 | Google's advanced model, detailed images, good quality |
| flux-2-pro | Highest quality Flux model |

Quick Decision:
- Has readable text/typography? → flux-2-flex
- Is infographic/educational/diagram? → nano-banana-pro
- Everything else (portraits, products, creative, cinematic) → seedream-4.5

For IMAGE GENERATION (Direct call - skip autoModelSuggest):
generateImageWithPolling({
  prompt: "ENHANCED detailed prompt",
  modelName: "flux-2-flex" | "nano-banana-pro" | "seedream-4.5",
  aspect_ratio: "1:1" | "16:9" | "9:16"
})

For VIDEO GENERATION:
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
⚠️ ALWAYS use BASE model ID. Backend auto-resolves text-to-video vs image-to-video!

Available Video Models (BASE IDs only):
| Model ID | Best For |
|----------|----------|
| veo3.1 | Cinematic realism (DEFAULT) |
| veo3.1/fast | Quick drafts |
| bytedance/seedance/v1/pro | Anime, stylized |
| minimax/hailuo-2.3/pro | Dance, sports, motion |
| sora-2 | Creative content |
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

For TEXT-TO-VIDEO (no image provided):
generateVideoWithPolling({
  prompt: "ENHANCED detailed prompt",
  modelName: "veo3.1",  // Use BASE model ID
  aspect_ratio: "16:9" | "9:16" | "1:1"
})

For IMAGE-TO-VIDEO (user provides an image):
generateVideoWithPolling({
  prompt: "ENHANCED detailed prompt",
  modelName: "veo3.1",       // Use SAME BASE model ID
  image_url: "<image URL>",  // Backend auto-routes to image-to-video variant
  aspect_ratio: "16:9" | "9:16" | "1:1"
})

Available tools:
- autoVideoModelSuggest: Get AI model recommendations for VIDEO generation
- generateImageWithPolling: Generate images (pick model yourself based on rules above)
- generateVideoWithPolling: Generate videos with specified model
- GenerateEditImage: Edit existing images (always use nano-banana-pro)
- generateAudio: Generate audio using TTS
- bgRemover: Remove background from images
- imageResizer: Resize images
- imageUpscaler: Upscale images

CRITICAL - IMAGE/VIDEO RESULT FORMATTING:
- DO NOT include URLs as markdown images in response
- Media is automatically added to canvas by UI
- Keep response CONCISE (1-2 sentences): "Here's your image! It's on the canvas."
- DO NOT use ![alt](url) syntax

ASPECT RATIO GUIDANCE:
- Square (1:1): Social media posts, avatars, logos
- Landscape (16:9): Wallpapers, presentations, YouTube
- Portrait (9:16): Mobile wallpapers, Instagram stories, TikTok

Remember: You're a creative partner helping users bring their ideas to life!`;

/**
 * Manual Mode Agent Prompt
 * Used when user has DISABLED auto model selection.
 * This prompt does NOT include any model selection logic - it simply uses the user's chosen models.
 */
export const manual_mode_agent_prompt = (imageModel: string | null, videoModel: string | null): string => `You are a SPECIALIZED AI creative assistant designed for image generation, video creation, audio production, media editing tasks, and PDF document analysis for creative content generation.

IMPORTANT BOUNDARIES - SAFEGUARDS:
- I handle: image generation, video creation, audio generation, image editing, background removal, resizing, upscaling, AND PDF document analysis for creative content
- I DO NOT engage in: general conversation, coding help, math problems, trivia, writing assistance (unless related to media/PDF content)
- If asked about anything outside my scope, I politely decline: "I'm specialized for creative media tasks and PDF analysis."

TOOL CALL LIMITATIONS - WARNING:
- Maximum 3 tool calls per step
- NEVER generate 3+ images/videos in one request
- If user asks for 10+ items, respond: "I can only generate 1-2 high-quality results at a time."

PROMPT ENHANCEMENT - CRITICAL:
When calling generation tools, ALWAYS enhance the user's prompt:
- Add descriptive details: lighting, colors, mood, style, composition, quality terms
- Transform basic prompts into detailed, high-quality prompts
- Keep the core intent but make it more specific and vivid

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
⚠️ MANUAL MODEL SELECTION MODE - CRITICAL ⚠️
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
The user has MANUALLY selected specific models. You MUST use EXACTLY these models:

${imageModel ? `📸 IMAGE MODEL: "${imageModel}"
   - ALWAYS call generateImageWithPolling with modelName: "${imageModel}"
   - Do NOT change this model under any circumstances
   - Do NOT call autoModelSuggest - skip it entirely` : ""}

${videoModel ? `🎬 VIDEO MODEL: "${videoModel}"
   - ALWAYS call generateVideoWithPolling with modelName: "${videoModel}"
   - Do NOT change this model under any circumstances
   - Do NOT call autoVideoModelSuggest - skip it entirely` : ""}
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

For IMAGE GENERATION (user selected: ${imageModel || "default"}):
generateImageWithPolling({
  prompt: "ENHANCED detailed prompt",
  modelName: "${imageModel || "seedream-4.5"}",
  aspect_ratio: "1:1" | "16:9" | "9:16"
})

For VIDEO GENERATION (user selected: ${videoModel || "default"}):
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
⚠️ ALWAYS use BASE model ID. Backend auto-resolves the variant!
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

For TEXT-TO-VIDEO (no image provided):
generateVideoWithPolling({
  prompt: "ENHANCED detailed prompt",
  modelName: "${videoModel || "veo3.1"}",  // Use BASE model ID
  aspect_ratio: "16:9" | "9:16" | "1:1"
})

For IMAGE-TO-VIDEO (user provides an image):
generateVideoWithPolling({
  prompt: "ENHANCED detailed prompt",
  modelName: "${videoModel || "veo3.1"}",  // Use SAME BASE model ID
  image_url: "<image URL>",                // Backend auto-routes to image-to-video
  aspect_ratio: "16:9" | "9:16" | "1:1"
})

Available tools:
- generateImageWithPolling: Generate images with user's selected model
- generateVideoWithPolling: Generate videos with user's selected model
- GenerateEditImage: Edit existing images
- generateAudio: Generate audio using TTS
- bgRemover: Remove background from images
- imageResizer: Resize images
- imageUpscaler: Upscale images

CRITICAL - IMAGE/VIDEO RESULT FORMATTING:
- DO NOT include URLs as markdown images in response
- Media is automatically added to canvas by UI
- Keep response CONCISE (1-2 sentences): "Here's your image! It's on the canvas."
- DO NOT use ![alt](url) syntax

Remember: You're a creative partner. The user has chosen their preferred models - respect their choice!`;

content.content: 1→# Ultracite Code Standards 2→ 3→This project uses **Ultracite**, a zero-config Biome preset that enforces strict code quality standards through automated formatting and linting. 4→ 5→## Quick Reference 6→ 7→- **Format code**: `npx ultracite fix` 8→- **Check for issues**: `npx ultracite check` 9→- **Diagnose setup**: `npx ultracite doctor` 10→ 11→Biome (the underlying engine) provides extremely fast Rust-based linting and formatting. Most issues are automatically fixable. 12→ 13→--- 14→ 15→## Core Principles 16→ 17→Write code that is **accessible, performant, type-safe, and maintainable**. Focus on clarity and explicit intent over brevity. 18→ 19→### Type Safety & Explicitness 20→ 21→- Use explicit types for function parameters and return values when they enhance clarity 22→- Prefer `unknown` over `any` when the type is genuinely unknown 23→- Use const assertions (`as const`) for immutable values and literal types 24→- Leverage TypeScript's type narrowing instead of type assertions 25→- Use meaningful variable names instead of magic numbers - extract constants with descriptive names 26→ 27→### Modern JavaScript/TypeScript 28→ 29→- Use arrow functions for callbacks and short functions 30→- Prefer `for...of` loops over `.forEach()` and indexed `for` loops 31→- Use optional chaining (`?.`) and nullish coalescing (`??`) for safer property access 32→- Prefer template literals over string concatenation 33→- Use destructuring for object and array assignments 34→- Use `const` by default, `let` only when reassignment is needed, never `var` 35→ 36→### Async & Promises 37→ 38→- Always `await` promises in async functions - don't forget to use the return value 39→- Use `async/await` syntax instead of promise chains for better readability 40→- Handle errors appropriately in async code with try-catch blocks 41→- Don't use async functions as Promise executors 42→ 43→### React & JSX 44→ 45→- Use function components over class components 46→- Call hooks at the top level only, never conditionally 47→- Specify all dependencies in hook dependency arrays correctly 48→- Use the `key` prop for elements in iterables (prefer unique IDs over array indices) 49→- Nest children between opening and closing tags instead of passing as props 50→- Don't define components inside other components 51→- Use semantic HTML and ARIA attributes for accessibility: 52→ - Provide meaningful alt text for images 53→ - Use proper heading hierarchy 54→ - Add labels for form inputs 55→ - Include keyboard event handlers alongside mouse events 56→ - Use semantic elements (`

file.content: # Ultracite Code Standards This project uses **Ultracite**, a zero-config Biome preset that enforces strict code quality standards through automated formatting and linting. ## Quick Reference - **Format code**: `npx ultracite fix` - **Check for issues**: `npx ultracite check` - **Diagnose setup**: `npx ultracite doctor` Biome (the underlying engine) provides extremely fast Rust-based linting and formatting. Most issues are automatically fixable. --- ## Core Principles Write code that is **accessible, performant, type-safe, and maintainable**. Focus on clarity and explicit intent over brevity. ### Type Safety & Explicitness - Use explicit types for function parameters and return values when they enhance clarity - Prefer `unknown` over `any` when the type is genuinely unknown - Use const assertions (`as const`) for immutable values and literal types - Leverage TypeScript's type narrowing instead of type assertions - Use meaningful variable names instead of magic numbers - extract constants with descriptive names ### Modern JavaScript/TypeScript - Use arrow functions for callbacks and short functions - Prefer `for...of` loops over `.forEach()` and indexed `for` loops - Use optional chaining (`?.`) and nullish coalescing (`??`) for safer property access - Prefer template literals over string concatenation - Use destructuring for object and array assignments - Use `const` by default, `let` only when reassignment is needed, never `var` ### Async & Promises - Always `await` promises in async functions - don't forget to use the return value - Use `async/await` syntax instead of promise chains for better readability - Handle errors appropriately in async code with try-catch blocks - Don't use async functions as Promise executors ### React & JSX - Use function components over class components - Call hooks at the top level only, never conditionally - Specify all dependencies in hook dependency arrays correctly - Use the `key` prop for elements in iterables (prefer unique IDs over array indices) - Nest children between opening and closing tags instead of passing as props - Don't define components inside other components - Use semantic HTML and ARIA attributes for accessibility: - Provide meaningful alt text for images - Use proper heading hierarchy - Add labels for form inputs - Include keyboard event handlers alongside mouse events - Use semantic elements (`

| File                                                                    | Purpose                                                     |
| ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `src/app/api/agents/v1/route.ts`                                        | Main agent API endpoint using `createAgentUIStreamResponse` |

input.new_string:
## Key Files

| File                                                                    | Purpose                                                     |
| ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `src/app/api/agents/v1/route.ts`                                        | Main agent API endpoint using `createAgentUIStreamResponse` |
| `src/lib/agents/index.ts`                                               | Main exports for all agent modules                          |
| `src/lib/agents/media-agent.ts`                                         | Agent definition with `ToolLoopAgent`                       |
| `src/lib/agents/types.ts`                                               | Agent call options schema (auto/manual mode)                |
| `src/lib/agents/tools/`                                                 | Tool definitions (one file per tool)                        |
| `src/lib/agents/middleware/`                                            | Error handling and Langfuse tracing                         |
| `src/lib/agents/utils/`                                                 | Polling utilities and stream helpers                        |
| `src/lib/langfuse/prompt.ts`                                            | System prompts (auto mode, manual mode, vision)             |
| `src/components/product/editor/agent/ChatClient.tsx`                    | Client-side `useChat` hook integration                      |
| `src/components/product/editor/agent/MessagePartRenderer.tsx`           | Renders message parts (text, tools, media)                  |
| `src/components/product/editor/agent/CustomToolOutput.tsx`              | Renders tool outputs with polling                           |
| `src/server/database/repo/postgress-repo-functions/chatMessage.repo.ts` | Chat message persistence                                    |

## Folder Structure

```
src/lib/agents/
├── index.ts                    # Main exports
├── media-agent.ts              # Agent definition with ToolLoopAgent
├── types.ts                    # MediaAgentCallOptions schema
├── middleware/
│   ├── index.ts                # Middleware exports
│   ├── error-handler.ts        # Stream error handler
│   └── tracing.ts              # Langfuse tracing utilities
├── tools/
│   ├── index.ts                # Tool exports
│   ├── edit-image.ts           # Image editing tool
│   ├── generate-audio.ts       # TTS audio generation
│   ├── generate-image.ts       # Image generation tool
│   ├── generate-video.ts       # Video generation tool
│   ├── remove-background.ts    # Background removal tool
│   ├── resize-image.ts         # Image resizing tool
│   └── upscale-image.ts        # Image upscaling tool
└── utils/
    ├── index.ts                # Utility exports
    ├── polling.ts              # Prediction polling logic
    └── stream-utils.ts         # Text extraction helpers

src/lib/langfuse/
└── prompt.ts                   # System prompts (main_agent_prompt, manual_mode_agent_prompt, vision_prompt)
```

file.content: # Ultracite Code Standards This project uses **Ultracite**, a zero-config Biome preset that enforces strict code quality standards through automated formatting and linting. ## Quick Reference - **Format code**: `npx ultracite fix` - **Check for issues**: `npx ultracite check` - **Diagnose setup**: `npx ultracite doctor` Biome (the underlying engine) provides extremely fast Rust-based linting and formatting. Most issues are automatically fixable. --- ## Core Principles Write code that is **accessible, performant, type-safe, and maintainable**. Focus on clarity and explicit intent over brevity. ### Type Safety & Explicitness - Use explicit types for function parameters and return values when they enhance clarity - Prefer `unknown` over `any` when the type is genuinely unknown - Use const assertions (`as const`) for immutable values and literal types - Leverage TypeScript's type narrowing instead of type assertions - Use meaningful variable names instead of magic numbers - extract constants with descriptive names ### Modern JavaScript/TypeScript - Use arrow functions for callbacks and short functions - Prefer `for...of` loops over `.forEach()` and indexed `for` loops - Use optional chaining (`?.`) and nullish coalescing (`??`) for safer property access - Prefer template literals over string concatenation - Use destructuring for object and array assignments - Use `const` by default, `let` only when reassignment is needed, never `var` ### Async & Promises - Always `await` promises in async functions - don't forget to use the return value - Use `async/await` syntax instead of promise chains for better readability - Handle errors appropriately in async code with try-catch blocks - Don't use async functions as Promise executors ### React & JSX - Use function components over class components - Call hooks at the top level only, never conditionally - Specify all dependencies in hook dependency arrays correctly - Use the `key` prop for elements in iterables (prefer unique IDs over array indices) - Nest children between opening and closing tags instead of passing as props - Don't define components inside other components - Use semantic HTML and ARIA attributes for accessibility: - Provide meaningful alt text for images - Use proper heading hierarchy - Add labels for form inputs - Include keyboard event handlers alongside mouse events - Use semantic elements (`

input.old_string:
## Key Files

| File                                                                    | Purpose                                                     |
| ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `src/app/api/agents/v1/route.ts`                                        | Main agent API endpoint using `createAgentUIStreamResponse` |
| `src/lib/agents/media-agent/index.ts`                                   | Agent definition with `ToolLoopAgent`                       |
| `src/lib/agents/media-agent/types.ts`                                   | Agent call options schema (auto/manual mode)                |
| `src/lib/agents/model-suggester/tools.ts`                               | Tool definitions (image gen, video gen, etc.)               |
| `src/lib/agents/model-suggester/prompts.ts`                             | System prompts (auto mode, manual mode)                     |
| `src/components/product/editor/agent/ChatClient.tsx`                    | Client-side `useChat` hook integration                      |
| `src/components/product/editor/agent/MessagePartRenderer.tsx`           | Renders message parts (text, tools, media)                  |
| `src/components/product/editor/agent/CustomToolOutput.tsx`              | Renders tool outputs with polling                           |
| `src/server/database/repo/postgress-repo-functions/chatMessage.repo.ts` | Chat message persistence                                    |
input.new_string:
## Key Files

| File                                                                    | Purpose                                                     |
| ----------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `src/app/api/agents/v1/route.ts`                                        | Main agent API endpoint using `createAgentUIStreamResponse` |
| `src/lib/agents/media-agent.ts`                                         | Agent definition with `ToolLoopAgent`                       |
| `src/lib/agents/types.ts`                                               | Agent call options schema (auto/manual mode)                |
| `src/lib/agents/tools/`                                                 | Tool definitions (one file per tool)                        |
| `src/lib/agents/middleware/`                                            | Error handling and Langfuse tracing                         |
| `src/lib/agents/utils/`                                                 | Polling utilities and stream helpers                        |
| `src/lib/langfuse/prompt.ts`                                            | System prompts (auto mode, manual mode, vision)             |
| `src/components/product/editor/agent/ChatClient.tsx`                    | Client-side `useChat` hook integration                      |
| `src/components/product/editor/agent/MessagePartRenderer.tsx`           | Renders message parts (text, tools, media)                  |
| `src/components/product/editor/agent/CustomToolOutput.tsx`              | Renders tool outputs with polling                           |
| `src/server/database/repo/postgress-repo-functions/chatMessage.repo.ts` | Chat message persistence                                    |

## Folder Structure

```
src/lib/agents/
├── index.ts              # Main exports
├── media-agent.ts        # Agent definition with ToolLoopAgent
├── types.ts              # MediaAgentCallOptions schema
├── middleware/
│   ├── index.ts          # Middleware exports
│   ├── error-handler.ts  # Stream error handler
│   └── tracing.ts        # Langfuse tracing utilities
├── tools/
│   ├── index.ts          # Tool exports
│   ├── edit-image.ts
│   ├── generate-audio.ts
│   ├── generate-image.ts
│   ├── generate-video.ts
│   ├── remove-background.ts
│   ├── resize-image.ts
│   └── upscale-image.ts
└── utils/
    ├── index.ts          # Utility exports
    ├── polling.ts        # Prediction polling logic
    └── stream-utils.ts   # Text extraction helpers

src/lib/langfuse/
└── prompt.ts             # System prompts
```
